items.q: What are the three primary colours in paint (subtractive)?
items.correct: Red, yellow, and blue (or magenta, yellow, cyan in print).
items.why: Students confuse light (additive: RGB) with paint/print (subtractive: RYB or CMY). RGB mixes light to white; RYB/CMY mixes pigment toward black - different colour models, different primaries.

items.q: What is the significance of Duchamp's 'Fountain'?
items.correct: It challenged the definition of art - the artist's choice, not their skill, becomes the artwork.
items.random_wrong: It is a study of plumbing.
items.why: Students miss the conceptual point. Duchamp didn't make the urinal - he selected and presented it. The radical claim is that ART IS DESIGNATION, opening the door to all conceptual art.

items.correct: Strong contrast between light and dark to model three-dimensional form.
items.why: Students think it's about subject matter (night scenes). Chiaroscuro is a TECHNIQUE using value contrast to suggest volume - exemplified by Caravaggio but used since the Renaissance to model form.

items.q: Whose triadic sign distinguishes icon, index and symbol?
items.why: Correct answer: Peirce. “Saussure” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Whose 1964 Rhetoric of the Image analysed Panzani's pasta ad?
items.why: Correct answer: Barthes. “Metz” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Mitchell publish Picture Theory?
items.why: The real figure is 1994 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
